title: Records Request

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFQ# 398393 , 2 
Title/Description: Metro Nashville Public Schools Camera System for Bus Fleet

Issuing Entity: Metro Nashville Public Schools, Purchasing Department

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions and cite the specific statutory basis for each withholding or redaction. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment. 

 Regards,
Jon Smith

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Jon Smith.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all records reflecting data collected and recorded by the Central Marin Police Authority in accordance with the California Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) from January 1, 2025, through the present. Please provide the raw data in excel or csv format.

2) Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of, the City/Town that pertain to any RIPA data that has been collected by the Police Department since January 1, 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

title: Supplement company correspondence (Food and Drug Administration)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Supplement company correspondence (Food and Drug Administration)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Email or letters containing warning letters, recall requests, 483s, or untitled letters between FDA's CDER or ORA and the following companies referencing yellow oleander contamination in supplements between January 1, 2023 to December 31, 2025

OBC Group, SdB Semilla de Brasil Tejocote Root, SdB ELITE, natural vida, SiluetaYa LLC, New Me, Shopnewmeroot.com, vidaslim co., vidaslim USA, Creativa Interiors, LLC, Privit Wellness, LLC, Innovacion Natural, LLC, World Green Nutrition Inc., Alipotec, Alipotec South TX, NWL Nutra, Global Mix Inc., Eva Nutrition, Sunset Sales +, ELV King, ALIPOTEC RAIZ DE TEJOCOTE, Natural Supplements, LLC, Science of Alpha, H and Natural, H & Natural, Backstage CTC Seller, Nutraholics ELV, Niwali, or Pastor-Villareal, Inc.

I am an investigative journalist writing for MedShadow.org.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Yasinski

communications.subject: Request FDA-FOIA-2026-865-HFP-1
communications.communication:
FDA-FOIA-2026-865-HFP-1 Dear: Emma Yasinski The attached record(s) are being provided by the U.S. Food & Drug Administration’s (FDA), Human Foods Program (HFP) in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, referenced above, for record(s) from the Food and Drug Administration pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act regarding: Email or letters containing warning letters, recall requests, 483s, or untitled letters between FDA's CDER or ORA and the following companies referencing yellow oleander contamination in supplements between January 1, 2023, to December 31, 2025. OBC Group, SdB Semilla de Brasil Tejocote Root, SdB ELITE, natural vida, SiluetaYa LLC, New Me, Shopnewmeroot.com, vidaslim co., vidaslim USA, Creativa Interiors, LLC, Privit Wellness, LLC, Innovacion Natural, LLC, World Green Nutrition Inc., Alipotec, Alipotec South TX, NWL Nutra, Global Mix Inc., Eva Nutrition, Sunset Sales +, ELV King, ALIPOTEC RAIZ DE TEJOCOTE, Natural Supplements, LLC, Science of Alpha, H and Natural, H & Natural, Backstage CTC Seller, Nutraholics ELV, Niwali, or Pastor-Villareal, Inc.. Your request is granted in full. We are enclosing 16 pages with this response. This is HFP’s final response to your FOIA request. If you have any questions about this response, you may contact me at kimberly.jones@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:kimberly.jones@fda.hhs.gov) or by phone 240-402-1825. 
Please note, your request is assigned to additional office(s) within FDA. A separate response will be sent by the additional FDA office(s). In accordance with 45 CFR § 5.61 and 21 CFR § 20.41(b)(5), you have the right to appeal this determination. Your appeal should clearly identify the agency determination that is being appealed. It would be helpful if you provide specific reasons explaining why you believe the agency's adverse determination should be reconsidered. By filing an appeal, you preserve your rights under FOIA and give the agency a chance to review and reconsider your request and the agency’s decision. Your appeal must be mailed or emailed within 90 days from the date of this response, to: Director, Office of Management and Enterprise Services U.S. Food & Drug Administration 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050 Rockville, MD 20857 FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) . Please clearly mark both the envelope and your letter “FDA Freedom of Information Act Appeal.” Items arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time will be deemed received on the next workday. If you would like to discuss our response before filing an appeal to attempt to resolve your dispute without going through the appeals process, please contact Ms. Charis Wilson at 240-402-9116. You may also contact the FDA FOIA Public Liaison for assistance at: Director, Office of Management and Enterprise Services, US Food & Drug Administration, 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050, Rockville, MD 20857, email: FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) . If you are unable to resolve your FOIA dispute through our FOIA Public Liaison, the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS), the Federal FOIA Ombudsman’s office, offers mediation services to help resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road–OGIS, College Park, MD 20740-6001; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769; email at ogis@nara.gov (mailto:ogis@nara.gov) . The following charges for this request to date may be included in a monthly invoice. Please do not submit payment unless you receive an invoice 
Reproduction = $ 0.00;   Search = $ 0.00;   Review = $0.00;   Other = $ 0.00;   Total = $0.00 Sincerely, 
Kimberly Jones Government Information Specialist Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) & Privacy Act (PA) 
On Detail to Office of Operations/OMES Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS) U. S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Human Foods Program (HFP) Office of Policy, Regulations & Information (OPRI) Telephone: 240-402-1825

title: NOVA CC - FOIA Request

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (Va. Code §2.2-3700 et seq.), I am requesting a dataset containing course grade distributions for Northern Virginia Community College.

Specifically, I request records for each course section (from the last 5 years) by academic term including:
Semester and Year of Course
Subject code and course number
Course title
Section number
Instructor name
Number of students receiving each grade (A, B, C, D, F, W, etc.)
Total enrollment
Average GPA for the section (if available)

If possible, I request that the data be provided in a machine-readable format such as CSV or Excel.
If your office is not the custodian of these records, please forward this request to the appropriate office.

Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with John Dowell.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Nash:

I am the FOIA officer for Northern Virginia Community College, and I am in receipt of your request submitted under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act seeking grade distribution for each course at NOVA.

NOVA charges a fee of $25 per hour for FOIA services. (See our FOIA page for details<https://www.nvcc.edu/about/offices/system-counsel/foia.html>.) This request is likely to take a significant amount of time to fulfill, so I am invoking the need for additional time as allowed by the statute ( §2.2-3704.B.4<https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter37/section2.2-3704/>). Once I hear back from you that you are willing to pay associated FOIA fees, I will move forward with your request.

Don't hesitate to reach out to FOIA@nvcc.edu<mailto:FOIA@nvcc.edu> should you have questions.

Sincerely,

KATHY A. THOMPSON
Assoc. Director of Business Communications
   & College FOIA Officer
Business Engagement
Office of Strategy, Research and Workforce Innovation
4001 Wakefield Chapel Road, Rm. 368, Annandale, VA 22003
Office: 703.323.2168 | Cell: 703.855.5556
KAThompson@nvcc.edu<mailto:KAThompson@nvcc.edu>  |  nvcc.edu<http://www.nvcc.edu/>

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Thompson,

Thank you for your response.

To help minimize the time and cost required to fulfill this request, I would like to narrow the scope. If possible, please provide data for the past three academic years instead of five. Please let me know whether this change would reduce the cost of fulfilling the request.

Additionally, if the requested information already exists in a precompiled format (such as internal reports, dashboards, or database exports), I would be happy to receive those existing records rather than requesting that a new dataset be created.

Before proceeding, could you please provide an estimate of the expected cost to fulfill the request, as well as the approximate number of hours required?

Thank you for your assistance.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Nash:

Your FOIA request to Northern Virginia Community College for grade distribution for each course section across five years, took 12 staff hours to complete, so the cost will be $300. Please advise as to whether you are willing to pay the FOIA fees for this request, and I can release the record.

Sincerely,

Kathy

KATHY A. THOMPSON
Assoc. Director of Business Communications
   and College FOIA Officer
Business Engagement & Career Services
Office of Strategy, Research and Workforce Innovation

title: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the FOIA Records Request. I would like to request both that a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every facility managed or owned by West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation:

* Overall staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Overall staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Overall population count (per prison, per month)
* Total number of infractions (per prison, per month)
* Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) (per prison, per month)
* Total number in restricted housing (per prison, per month)
* Total number of appeals from incarcerated individuals (per prison, per month)

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility managed or owned by West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation. In order to help to determine my status to assess fees: I am affiliated with an educational institution, and this request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessie Harney

title: AER - CO Dept. of Public Health and Env.

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: AER - CO Dept. of Public Health and Env.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse event records filed with the Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Nolan

title: Pennsylvania HHS response

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Pennsylvania HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Pennsylvania Department of Health remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Pennsylvania Department of Health employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: DOH - RTKL
communications.communication:
The Pennsylvania Department of Health's Right-to-Know Law (RTKL) Office has received your correspondence. Requests for records will be processed in accordance with the provisions of the RTKL, 65 P.S.§§67.101, et seq.

The Department is deemed to have received the request on the business day that the AORO actually receives the request. See 65 P.S. § 67.901. This includes after regular business hours (5 pm) or when the Office is otherwise closed pursuant to Management Directive 530.17 and will be marked as received on the next business day.

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Archie Smith

title: Records Request

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law, I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP# 25-19

Title/Description: School Bus Stop-Arm Camera Program

Issuing Entity: Town of Hamden

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

The number of buses installed and number of tickets issued from the beginning of the contract for the school bus program.  

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 


Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

communications.subject: IPRA Request
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The responsive records to the attached request have been completed.  Due to the large file, these records cannot be sent through email.  We will need to download the records onto a thumb drive for a charge of $10.00.  We have 2 options for this process:

Option 1: We download the records onto a thumb drive and have it ready for pick-up here at our office at a scheduled time you choose.  Cost: $10.00

Option 2: We download the records onto a thumb drive and send it through the mail directly to your office.  Before we proceed with this option, we will need a check sent to us for the thumb drive and return postage. Cost: $22.00 ($10.00 for thumb drive & $12.00 for return postage) Address for check to be sent: 660 Main St. NW Los Lunas, NM 87031 (Address check to The Village of Los Lunas)  You can address the envelope to The Village of Los Lunas (Attn: Mykayla Moody)

Let me know what option works best for you, and we will begin the process as soon as possible.


Mykayla Moody 😊
Village of Los Lunas
Administrative Assistant
Phone: 505-839-3840
E-Mail: moodym@loslunasnm.gov

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: MD mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Internal evaluation or pilot assessment reports that summarize performance, adoption, risks, or “lessons learned” from Maryland’s mDL program (Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, Samsung Wallet, Maryland Mobile ID for Business) between January 1, 2023 and the present.

Agreements, contracts, or memoranda of understanding with vendors or partners, including but not limited to Apple, Google, Samsung, IDEMIA, AAMVA, or other providers.

Interagency or interstate coordination documents, including but not limited to MOUs or correspondence with AAMVA or other states regarding mDL interoperability, standards development, or airport integration at Baltimore/Washington International Thurgood Marshall Airport (BWI).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: Your request has been submitted – Case No: PR-CN00005666
communications.communication:
MDOT MVA Public Records 
   
    
  
Hi Rebecca Williams, 
**This is an automated response that your request has been received. PIA requests will receive a response within 10 business days. Subpoena requests will generally receive a response two weeks prior to the trial or return date you provide.
 Please do NOT respond to this email as the inbox does not accept replies.** 
We look forward to serving you! MDOT MVA has a robust menu of online services that can be completed from your phone, tablet or computer. (https://mva.maryland.gov/online-services/Pages/default.aspx) Create a myMVA profile to take advantage of all that our online services have to offer! (https://mymva.maryland.gov/go/web/createmymvaprofile) 
For those customers that require an in-person visit to MDOT MVA, our offices are open by appointment Monday through Saturday (times vary, see our locations page). Make an appointment (https://mva.maryland.gov/locations/Pages/default.aspx) here. Customers that prefer to use our business kiosks and those that are making a tag return can walk-in to complete those services. VEIP tests are also available without an appointment. (https://license.mva.maryland.gov/CheckList/default.aspx) 
Thank you again for contacting the MDOT MVA. We appreciate hearing from you. 
- TEAM MDOT MVA

title: Untitled (Department of Justice, United States Marshals)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled (Department of Justice, United States Marshals)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any memos, emails, and/or policies since January 20, 2025, related to the effort between ATF, DEA, FBI, US Marshals, and HSI to locate and interview at-risk unaccompanied alien children who entered the United States over the past four years.

- Any memos, reports, and/or emails drafted since January 20, 2025 quantifying the number and/or percentage of children identified as abused, trafficked, neglected, or exploited through Unaccompanied Alien Children Operations and/or other HSI wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children. 

- All trainings, memos, and/or policies used since January 20, 2025 about communicating with children and/or using trauma-informed interviewing skills provided to agents completing wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lara S Nochomovitz

title: H. Bynam - FOIA US Army Vietnam war

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: H. Bynam - FOIA US Army Vietnam war
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) concerning Holland E. Bynam
Name of Veteran: Holland Bynam
Branch: United States Army
DOB: July 26, 1935
Dates of Service: 1954 - 1984

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards, decorations, Badges, campaigns, assignments, Vietnam War service dates

4. Military Education/ training courses, military schools
5. copy of Bronze Star Medal award citations
6. request copy of Army commendation award citations
7 request copy of legion of merit award citation
8. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form
9. date of discharge
10: Overseas service date
I request copies of paperwork in the file showing this information

First name: Holland
Middle initial: E
Last name: Bynam
date of birth - 07/26/1935
Rank - Colonel
https://olg52.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/COL-H.E.-Bynam-1.jpg

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the Navy's Personnel Files (OMPF) for a former service member named Nicholas Joseph LaLota (US Navy, 2000 - 2008), DOB: 06/23/1978.

The public interest in Nicholas Joseph LaLota, who is running for political office this year in New York (https://lalota.house.gov/), should favor disclosure of many of these records, particularly in relation to any disciplinary records, should those records exist.

Please be sure to review and release any segregable information, even if certain documents or portions of documents are withheld.

PLEASE NOTE: I am not a next of kin of this servicemember, and thus do not know LaLota's Social Security Number. Because of this, I am ONLY requesting records releasable under the Freedom of Information Act. Please process my request for this servicemember's OMPF and other records under the Freedom of Information Act’s relevant provisions. I am not seeking any records that would be releasable under the Privacy Act.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. Furthermore, I am a member of the news media and this request is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin
A fix is required to perfect the request.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record
communications.communication:
Hello,

I would like to confirm that this response from the NPRC means that the search for this service member's personnel file was unsuccessful with the information I supplied: name, date of birth, dates of service and branch of military. The only information that leaves from the list provided is the service member's social security. Thus, I would like to further confirm that the search was unsuccessful using all the information I supplied and can only be completed with this service member's social security number. Is that correct?

Best,
Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record
communications.communication:
To whom it may concern,

This is an appeal under the Freedom of Information Act. My request was sent to the National Personnel Records Center on May 31, 2024, and I received a response on June 26, 2024. I requested a copy of the Navy's Personnel Files (OMPF) for a former service member named Nicholas Joseph LaLota. 

The response that I received on June 26, 2024, stated that the Center was not able to locate the records I requested and asked that I provide as many of the following as possible: the veteran's complete name, date and place of birth, service number, social security number, branch of service, approximate dates of service, place of enlistment and place of separation. I had already provided the officer's full name, branch, dates of service and date of birth. 

In my request, I stated "PLEASE NOTE: I am not a next of kin of this servicemember, and thus do not know LaLota's Social Security Number. Because of this, I am ONLY requesting records releasable under the Freedom of Information Act. Please process my request for this servicemember's OMPF and other records under the Freedom of Information Act’s relevant provisions. I am not seeking any records that would be releasable under the Privacy Act."

On June 26, 2024, I called the NPRC Customer Service Line to speak with a representative about what information I hadn't supplied and what information would be most critical for retrieving the records that I requested. The first representative I spoke with told me that she could see my request was unsuccessful and that I would need to provide the service member's social security number. I told her that I am requesting public records on a public official, and I am not next-of-kin, so I do not have his social security number. She said his social security number would be the only way to retrieve the documents. I asked her whether there was anything else she could tell me about my request and the attempted search. She said no. I hung up and then called back again to see if a different representative would be able to give me more context. But again, the representative told me that the Center couldn't tell me anything more than that I needed the service member's social security number. 

On July 3, 2024, I responded to my request by fax and asked the Center to confirm whether the information I already supplied was not enough and whether this means they require a social security number. As of August 14, 2024, I have not received a reply. 

Only July 15, I emailed the Center's FOIA outreach manager, Lisa M. Sandor. Sandor and I's email communication is attached to this appeal as a PDF. Sandor reiterated what I heard from employees on the phone: that she couldn't tell me more about the search associated with request, beyond that I would just need a social security number. 

I believe I have demonstrated due diligence and attempted to work with the NRPC to refine or clarify my search for records, but have received inadequate response from NRPC employees. I have not received a clear explanation as to why a social security number is needed in this instance and whether there are any other ways that I can improve my request to result in a successful search without the service member's social security number. I am concerned that if public records on a public official are only public if the requester has access to that public official's social security number, those records are effectively not public to begin with.  

Furthermore, there is strong public interest in Nicholas Joseph LaLota, who is running for political office this year in New York  (https://lalota.house.gov/) and that interest should favor disclosure of many of these records, particularly in relation to any disciplinary records, should those records exist. The right to know and verify a politician's claims about military service is an essential tool for journalists. If these records require a service member's social security number, the records are inaccessible to the media. 

If you need to discuss this request, I can be reached at (267) 314-7952. Thank you for your consideration of this appeal.

My best,
Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: E & A Occupancy + Fire Sprinkler Information (Eagle Pass City Council)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request an electronic, parcel-level extract containing current Group E (Educational) and Group A (Assembly) occupancies and associated fire-sprinkler information for all parcels within the jurisdiction: 

Please provide one CSV with the following columns (your native field names are fine):
- Parcel identifier (APN / PIN / BBL)
• Site address (street #, street, city, ZIP)
• Latitude & longitude
• Internal building / permit ID
• Year built
• Floor area (sq ft)
• Number of stories
• Current IBC occupancy code (E, A-1…A-5)
• Occupancy description
• Fire-sprinkler present (Yes / No / Unknown)
• Date of last fire-sprinkler inspection (if tracked)
• Owner name & mailing address (if stored)

If any field is not maintained, please note that rather than withholding the entire record.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Cavise

title: Response to Medical Emergencies - Connecticut Department of Correction

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Response to Medical Emergencies - Connecticut Department of Correction
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All written policies and procedures of the Connecticut Department of Correction governing responses to medical emergencies, including policies related to the transport of incarcerated individuals to community hospitals or other external medical facilities.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophie Choong

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Pennsylvania Department of State)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Pennsylvania Department of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Marines support for NCIS, Chopped, XVII Carvings, The Ultimate Fighter, To What Remains, and The Price Is Right

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for Food Network reality TV series "Chopped"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the Food Network reality TV series "Chopped." According to a document released to me by the Marines through FOIA (see link below), the Marines provided support to this production. I therefore request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "Chopped," including all episodes with which the Marines were involved. Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Chopped," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

Here is a link to the aforementioned document:

https://app.box.com/s/t7tbqia68nc136qisb88aw4yvsoajzem

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Information on Flock Saftey and AI-assisted Cameras

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Information on Flock Saftey and AI-assisted Cameras
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The location of all Flock Safety or other AI-assisted cameras installed in the last 90 days (please share locations as map coordinates or a street address with nearest cross-street)
- The number of Flock Safety or other AI-assisted cameras planned to be installed and the location where JPD plans to put them (please share locations as map coordinates or a street address with nearest cross-street)
- All email communications with Immigration and Customs Enforcement regarding data sharing from Flock Safety cameras
- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety automated license plate readers (ALPRs) over the last 30 days
- The names of the hot lists/watch lists used with Flock Safety automated license plate readers
- The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- All agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
- All contracts, MOUs, or other agreements with Flock Safety
- All procurement documents related to Flock Safety

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen

title: Deaths-in-custody

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Deaths-in-custody
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The database or records of all Illinois law enforcement agencies deaths-in-custody up to the date this request is ultimately fulfilled, including all deaths-in-custody reported pursuant both Illinois state legislation (730 ILCS 210/3-5) and federal legislation (34 U.S.C. § 60105), as well as all data dictionaries, database scheme documentation or other documentation on the collection and organizatin of the data. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska" (2020-2023). Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Ice Airport Alaska," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Ice Airport Alaska" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

title: Clark County Court Administrator of the District Court Jury List Request

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Clark County Court Administrator of the District Court Jury List Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to NV Rev Stat § 6.045(5)(c)(1)

The following materials regarding the compilation and composition of the state’s master jury list:

All current policies and procedures regarding how the current master jury list was compiled and how often master jury lists are reviewed and updated. Please name all source lists (driver’s license records, voter registration lists, etc) that were utilized in creating the current master jury list.

All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding the composition of the current master jury list. Please include the total number of individuals on the current master jury list and all relevant demographic data, including but not limited to the race, age and gender of individuals that comprise the current master jury list.

In the event that there are any fees for obtaining or copying these records, please inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. Should the fees exceed $100, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees as I am requesting the information on behalf of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how master jury lists are created. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
If possible, please provide the requested documents electronically. However, if this is not feasible, please inform me of the available methods for obtaining these records. Further, if you expect a significant delay in responding to this request, please contact me with information concerning when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.
If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within the timeframe required by the statute.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Elijah Salisbury

communications.communication:
Thank you so much for everything you've sent over. 

I was wondering if there was any explanation for some of the missing race data. The "Unknown" column is marked "Yes" for a plethora of potential jurors across the shared data (about 44% across the shared years). Is this due to participants not filling this portion out during the summons process or another occurrence?

Looking forward to hearing from you soon.

Thank you in advance, 
Elijah

communications.subject: Record Request Ticket #12351
communications.communication:
Good morning.  I am responding to your follow-up question regarding the "unknown" column in the jury report that was provided to you.  I have confirmed that the "Yes" designations in those fields denote when a prospective juror declines to make a selection.

Please do not hesitate to reach out if I can be of further assistance.

[Signature]
This email may contain legally privileged and/or confidential information. If you are not the intended recipient(s), any dissemination, distribution or copying of this email message is strictly prohibited. If you have received this message in error, please immediately notify the sender and delete this email from your computer.

title: Prisacts

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Prisacts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records captioned "Prisacts" or "Revolutionary Activities in Prisons", including file 62-HQ-116018 and any bulky files, EBFs, or sub files if they exist.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.communication:
Dear Ivan: 
 
Thank you for your interest in Chicago Public Schools. Your FOIA request has been submitted. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: N019250-031526 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

I would like to request and receive the most current LSC training records for all current LSC members across CPS. In asking for training records, I'm requesting the names of LSC members, their role/school/network, and the most current information as to whether or not they have completed the respective 9 LSC trainings, including any associated completion dates. (e.g., completion date, location, trainer). 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Ivan 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2240706/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019250-031526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
03/30/2026 
Dear Ivan Ivan: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019250-031526 
Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 
I would like to request and receive the most current LSC training records for all current LSC members across CPS. In asking for training records, I'm requesting the names of LSC members, their role/school/network, and the most current information as to whether or not they have completed the respective 9 LSC trainings, including any associated completion dates. (e.g., completion date, location, trainer). 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 
Ivan 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2240706/ 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Student information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Marisol Muneton Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Purchasing Policy

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The Town of Hotchkiss purchasing policy, procurement policy, bid policy, and any vendor or purchasing procedures, including any resolutions or ordinances that govern purchases and contract awards, effective September 2022, December 2024, and March 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lester Christensen

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Purchasing Policy
communications.communication:
Hello,

The Town's Purchasing Policy is not a criminal justice record and should be provided by CORA (not CCJRA), and the first hour of retrieval is free. 

Best practices and public transparency would have this policy on the Town's website. The County, the City of Delta, the library district and even the Town of Paonia have theirs freely available on their websites, such that a CORA request is not necessary. It would be nice to see Hotchkiss do the same. 

Please fulfill this request. 

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Purchasing Policy
communications.communication:
Hello,

It has been a week. Am I understanding correctly that the Town of Hotchkiss is not willing to freely provide its purchasing policy as per CORA? 

This is NOT a criminal justice record and should be provided as per CORA, and the first hour of retrieval is free. 

Again, best practices and public transparency would have this policy on the Town's website. The County, the City of Delta, the library district and even the Town of Paonia have theirs freely available on their websites, such that a CORA request is not necessary. 

It would be nice to see Hotchkiss do the same.

Please fulfill this request without further delay.

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #FOIL-2024-002-00272
communications.communication:
Dear Records Appeal Officer,

Two years ago, I submitted a request (FOIL-2024-002-00272) for emails and other records pertaining to the New York City Fair Workweek Law. These records were previously requested in 2016 and 2017 (FOIL-2016-002-00410, FOIL-2017-002-00582) and subsequently posted to the OpenRecords portal, according to the closing messages dated 12/26/2017. For reasons that are still unclear to me, none of these records are publicly available on the so-called OpenRecords portal. 

After seven missed deadlines and extensions, I received a letter (see attached) from Records Access Officer Megha Jain on March 19, 2026, informing me that the Mayor’s Office identified records responsive to my request. The letter states that "The responsive records are attached." However, the letter is a single-page PDF with no attachments. I followed the instructions in the accompanying email (from: donotreply@records.nyc.gov, to: 160724-04060059@requests.muckrock.com), logging into the OpenRecords account associated with this request. I see no records or attachments, not even the response letter, only the generic acknowledgement and extension notices. 

I am appealing the constructive denial of my request through the excessive delay and failure of the Mayor's Office to provide any responsive records. Note that I am using the MuckRock website to track this request and ensure that any records released are publicly accessible at the following webpage: https://www.muckrock.com/foi/new-york-city-17/previously-released-fair-workweek-emails-160724. Please respond in writing (to 160724-04060059@requests.muckrock.com) within ten business days of your receipt of my appeal, stating whether the request is granted or denied in full or in part. If the appeal is granted, please state a date certain by which the records requested will be produced. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter,
Peter Fugiel

title: Georgia Public Records Request – DeKalb County Sheriff – DeLozier Case

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Public Records Request – DeKalb County Sheriff – DeLozier Case
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To: Records Division, DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office 

Date: March 17, 2026

Re: Public Records Request – Administrative Records Concerning Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I respectfully request access to administrative, clerical, and procedural records maintained by the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office concerning the handling of incidents or actions involving myself, Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

This request is limited to administrative records and does not seek private or sensitive information about any other individual.

Requested Records:
1.  Incident Reports / Case Files
Administrative logs, internal notes, and summaries documenting incidents involving me.
Assignment records indicating which personnel reviewed or processed these incidents.
References to contractors, vendors, or third parties assisting in administrative handling.

2.  911 / Dispatch Records
Call logs, CAD entries, or summaries of dispatch activity referencing incidents involving me.
Metadata indicating times of receipt, personnel assigned, and administrative handling.

3.  Internal Communications / Administrative Memos
Emails, memoranda, or notes documenting the administrative handling, processing, or review of incidents involving me.
Documentation of decisions to close, escalate, or otherwise manage cases from a clerical or administrative perspective.

4.  Investigator / Deputy Assignments
Records showing which deputies or personnel were assigned to administrative handling of incidents involving me.
References to contractors or vendors who assisted in administrative work.

5.  Administrative Policies and Procedures
Written policies, procedures, or guidance governing the processing, review, and maintenance of incident records or dispatch logs.
Training materials or internal documentation relevant to clerical handling of cases.

6.  Metadata and Audit Trails
Timestamps, system-generated logs, or audit trails documenting access, review, or updates to administrative records concerning me.

While this request concerns myself as the requester, it is limited strictly to administrative and procedural records. It is submitted in the public interest to document the Sheriff’s Office handling of incidents and clerical actions. This request does not seek investigative opinions, confidential officer notes, or privileged material, and is therefore not subject to Privacy Act or investigative exemptions. The public record created by this submission establishes a transparent timeline of accountability and ensures that routine administrative handling cannot be withheld under claims of ongoing investigation.

A corresponding request for full records, including sensitive identifiers and information necessary for administrative review, will be submitted directly to the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office via USPS. This public-facing submission documents transparency and accountability, while the USPS submission ensures that all legally available records relating to my interactions with the Office are fully identified and obtained.
 
This request is submitted to document the administrative handling of incidents involving myself and to ensure transparency and public accountability in the operations of the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office.

Format and Fees:    Please provide records in electronic format if available. If any fees apply, please notify me in advance before processing.

Thank you for your attention and assistance in providing these public records.

communications.communication:
Subject: Follow Up: Open Records Request S000596-031726

I am writing to follow up on my Open Records Request (Reference No. S000596-031726), submitted on March 17, 2026.

While I received confirmation of receipt, I have not yet received a substantive response regarding the status of the request, including whether records will be produced, any applicable exemptions, or an estimated timeline for fulfillment, as contemplated under the Georgia Open Records Act.

I understand that certain records may be accessible through the County’s centralized request portal, which I have now accessed. However, to ensure a complete record, please confirm whether the Sheriff’s Office is processing this request directly and whether any responsive records are maintained by your office.

If any portion of my request has been redirected, deferred, or limited due to overlap with other departments, please clarify that in your response.

I would appreciate an update at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: Sheriff's Office Records Request :: S000596-031726

RE: Sheriff's Office Records Request of March 17, 2026, Reference # S000596-031726 Dear Charles DeLozier, The DeKalb County Sheriff's Office, GA received a public records request from you on March 17, 2026. Your request mentioned: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

"To: Records Division, DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office 

Date: March 17, 2026

Re: Public Records Request – Administrative Records Concerning Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I respectfully request access to administrative, clerical, and procedural records maintained by the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office concerning the handling of incidents or actions involving myself, Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

This request is limited to administrative records and does not seek private or sensitive information about any other individual.

Requested Records:
1. Incident Reports / Case Files
Administrative logs, internal notes, and summaries documenting incidents involving me.
Assignment records indicating which personnel reviewed or processed these incidents.
References to contractors, vendors, or third parties assisting in administrative handling.

2. 911 / Dispatch Records
Call logs, CAD entries, or summaries of dispatch activity referencing incidents involving me.
Metadata indicating times of receipt, personnel assigned, and administrative handling.

3. Internal Communications / Administrative Memos
Emails, memoranda, or notes documenting the administrative handling, processing, or review of incidents involving me.
Documentation of decisions to close, escalate, or otherwise manage cases from a clerical or administrative perspective.

4. Investigator / Deputy Assignments
Records showing which deputies or personnel were assigned to administrative handling of incidents involving me.
References to contractors or vendors who assisted in administrative work.

5. Administrative Policies and Procedures
Written policies, procedures, or guidance governing the processing, review, and maintenance of incident records or dispatch logs.
Training materials or internal documentation relevant to clerical handling of cases.

6. Metadata and Audit Trails
Timestamps, system-generated logs, or audit trails documenting access, review, or updates to administrative records concerning me.

While this request concerns myself as the requester, it is limited strictly to administrative and procedural records. It is submitted in the public interest to document the Sheriff’s Office handling of incidents and clerical actions. This request does not seek investigative opinions, confidential officer notes, or privileged material, and is therefore not subject to Privacy Act or investigative exemptions. The public record created by this submission establishes a transparent timeline of accountability and ensures that routine administrative handling cannot be withheld under claims of ongoing investigation.

A corresponding request for full records, including sensitive identifiers and information necessary for administrative review, will be submitted directly to the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office via USPS. This public-facing submission documents transparency and accountability, while the USPS submission ensures that all legally available records relating to my interactions with the Office are fully identified and obtained.

This request is submitted to document the administrative handling of incidents involving myself and to ensure transparency and public accountability in the operations of the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office.

Format and Fees:  Please provide records in electronic format if available. If any fees apply, please notify me in advance before processing.

Thank you for your attention and assistance in providing these public records.

Sincerely, 
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2242893/

The DeKalb County Sheriff's Office, GA has reviewed its files and has determined there are no responsive record(s) to your request.

For questions or additional information, please reply to this email.

Sincerely,

DeKalb County Sheriff's Office
Open Records Section"
