communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request #S000596-031726
communications.communication:
Dear DeKalb County Sheriff's Office Open Records Section

I am writing regarding my Open Records Request (Reference No. S000596-031726), submitted on March 17, 2026, and your response indicating that there are no responsive records. While I appreciate the response, to fully comply with the Georgia Open Records Act (O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.) and to document the steps taken in your search, I respectfully request the following detailed information regarding the search and determination of responsiveness:

1	Scope of Search: A description of all offices, departments, databases, and physical files reviewed in connection with this request.
	
2	Search Methodology: The criteria, keywords, date ranges, and procedures used in conducting the search, including any guidance or instructions provided to staff performing the search.
	
3	Records Determination: Clarification on whether any records were found but deemed exempt, partially withheld, or redirected, and the statutory basis for any exemptions applied.
	
4	Search Completeness: Confirmation that the search was conducted with reasonable diligence in accordance with the requirements of the Georgia Open Records Act, including any steps taken to ensure that responsive records were not overlooked.

This information is requested to understand the factual basis for your determination that no records are responsive, and to ensure that any subsequent inquiries or requests are accurately targeted. Providing these details is consistent with the purpose of the Georgia Open Records Act, which allows requesters to verify agency determinations and understand the processes used to locate records.

I respectfully request a response detailing the search process and determination factors at your earliest convenience. Documentation of this information will ensure that all parties have a clear and accurate record of your office’s compliance with the Act.

Thank you for your attention and cooperation.

Sincerely,
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

title: Financial Reports

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Financial Reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Greg Chick

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Chautauqua County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Chautauqua County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Radford University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Radford University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Parental Objections (Laconia School District/SAU30)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Parental Objections (Laconia School District/SAU30)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

All parent and/or guardian objections to instructional and library materials from this school district between the 2023-2024 and 2024-2025 school years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: ww2 reports request

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ww2 reports request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I am requesting access to and copies of the following historical intelligence reports regarding German underground installations, factories, and assembly plants during World War II.

These reports were generated by the Combined Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (CIOS), the British Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (BIOS), and the Joint Intelligence Objectives Agency (JIOA).
Requested Documents:

1. CIOS File Numbers:

    File No. II-12: Visit to Underground V-1 Manufacturing Plant (Sept 1944)

    File No. X-1: Caves at Bad Maries, used for Storage of Peroxide (Col. W.H. Wheeler)

    File No. XXIV-26, 27: Electronic Valve (Tube) Factories TeKaDe, Helmbrechts and Philips; Minden

    File No. XXVI-44: Messerschmidt Bombproof Assembly Plant Landsberg

    File No. XXVI-87: Dachs-I (Underground Lubricating Oil Plant) Porta, Westphalia

    File No. XXVII-59: Underground German Liquid Oxygen Plant, Wittring

    File No. XXVIII-45: Investigation of Group 2 Targets in Nordhausen Area

    File No. XXXII-15: Report on Visit to Kuckuck I Plant at Niedersachswerfen

    File No. XXXII-17: Underground Factories in Central Germany (Col. W.R.J. Cook)

2. CIOS Evaluation Reports:

    Evaluation Report No. 87: Investigation of Dannenberg V-1 Assembly Plant

    Evaluation Report No. 106: Target No. 28/5.55 Industriekontor, G.m.b.H.

    Evaluation Report No. 182: Underground Factory Sites in Ansbach

    Evaluation Report No. 238: Hauptausschuss Elektrotechnik (List of Underground Plants)

    Evaluation Report No. 252: Phillips Underground Valve Plant, Porta, Westfalia

3. JIOA Final Reports:

    Final Report No. 1: German Underground Installations. Part One: Unique Design and Construction

    Final Report No. 2: German Underground Installations. Part Two: Adaptations of Existing Facilities

    Final Report No. 3: German Underground Installations. Part Three: Various Installations

4. BIOS Final Reports:

    Final Report No. 65: Philips Valvo Works, Hamburg; Hammerwerke, Minden; C.H. Müller A.G., Hamburg

    Final Report No. 324: Band Oil Refinery at Porta (Nr. Minden)

Fee Waiver / Categorization

I am requesting these for personal research and historical study. I am willing to pay fees up to $[Insert Amount, e.g., $25.00], but please notify me if the costs will exceed this amount. Given the age of these documents (80+ years), I request that they be provided in digital format if available to minimize costs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tino Von Struckmann

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: ww2 reports request
communications.communication:
This is an automated reply to inform you that the National Archives and Records Administration, Transparency and Access Program (FOIA Program Area) has received your email.
We will respond within 20 working days. Please note that submitting duplicate requests may increase wait times. If you have any questions, you may call our office at 301-837-3190.

You can also visit our web page at www.archives.gov/research/foia.

communications.subject: Re: [26-31772] Freedom of Information Act Request - Tino Von Struckmann
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Struckmann:



This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act request for
"historical intelligence reports regarding German underground
installations, factories, and assembly plants during World War II." Your
request was received in this office on March 4, 2026, with the assigned
control number 26-31772



*As these records are not classified, they do not require a FOIA request
and are available for you or your representative to review for pertinent
records.* We make reasonable efforts to assist researchers with their work.
As a matter of policy, the National Archives is staffed to assist
researchers with their work, including but not limited to pointing
researchers to helpful finding aids to begin their research. However, given
the limitations on staff resources, we do not generally conduct substantive
research in response to requests filed under the Freedom of Information
Act, except in circumstances where documents are easily locatable.



British Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (BIOS), Combined Intelligence
Objectives Sub-committee (CIOS) and Joint Intelligence Objectives Agency
(JIOA) reports are not always filed by report number nor are the always
found in distinct record series of such reports. They can also be found
filed under report titles and/or by topic under broader subject headings.
BIOS, CIOS and JIOA reports can be found among the following record series:



Records of the War Department General and Special Staffs (Record Group
165), Publication Files (Entry NM84 79
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/1557240>);



Records of the Office of Scientific Research and Development (Record Group
227), Records of the London Mission, 1941-1946 (Entry NC138 176
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/6920098>) (under CIO subseries, file
“evaluation reports”)



Records of the U.S. Strategic Bombing Survey (Record Group 243), Security
Classified  European Survey Intelligence Library, 1939-1945 (Entry I10 36
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/561425>) (Envelope 881 contains target
priorities and reports prepared by BIOS during 1944-1945)



Records of the Army Staff (Record Group 319), Publication Files (Entry NM3
82 <https://catalog.archives.gov/id/656424>)



Records of Allied Operational and Occupation Headquarters, World War II
(Record Group 331), CIOS Reports, 1944-1945 (Entry UD 13-D
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/573950>)



Records of Headquarters, European Theater of Operations, United States Army
(Record Group 498), Records Relating to CIOS, 1944-1945 (Entry UD 1161
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/5898423>).



Among the Records of the Office of the Secretary of Defense (Record Group
330) are following record series which contain records of the Joint
Intelligence Objectives Agency (JIOA):



General Correspondence, 1947–1952 (Entry A1 1062-A
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/7450323>)

Foreign Scientist Case Files, 1947–1958 (Entry A1 1-B
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/7450328>)



The Imperial War Museum appears to have a significant collection of BIOS,
CIOS and JIOA reports among the records in its collection. A link to the
description of these records can be found here
<https://www.iwm.org.uk/collections/item/object/1030013221>.



If you have questions regarding the publicly available records recommended
above, you may contact us at archives2reference@nara.gov.  We hope you find
this information useful in conducting your research.



This concludes the processing of your request. We do not consider this a
denial of your FOIA request. If you are not satisfied with our action on
this request, your options for appeal and dispute resolution are attached.



Sincerely,



PAUL B. BROWN



Textual Reference Branch

National Archives at College Park, MD



Attachment

Dear Requester:  The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. Within the next ten (10) days, we will determine whether your request seeks copies of disclosable records in the City's possession or whether the City will require an extension. Sincerely,   City Clerk's Office (831) 768-3048 275 Main Street, Suite 400, Watsonville, CA 95076 FAX: 831-761-0736 E-mail:  cityclerk@watsonville.gov Open Monday - Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact City of Watsonville, CA.

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Watsonville, CA public records request #26-55
communications.communication:
City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-55: Dear Syd Jones, The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7290.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. The City is typically afforded ten (10 days) from the date of receipt of your request to make a determination whether your request seeks disclosable public records. In accordance with the California Public Records Act, pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535, the City will require an extension of that deadline of up to an additional fourteen (14) days to make such a determination because of the reasons checked as follows: [ ] The need to search for and collect the requested records from facilities separate from the office processing the request. [X] The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. [ ] The need for consultation with another department or agency that has substantial interest in the response to the request. [ ] The need to compile data, to write programming language or a computer program, or to construct a computer report to extract data. Accordingly, the City will determine whether it possesses records responsive to your Request on or before 2/20/2026. If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at the number or email listed below. Sincerely, City Clerk's Office (831) 768-3048 275 Main Street, Suite 400, Watsonville, CA 95076 FAX: 831-761-0736 E-mail:  cityclerk@watsonville.gov Open Monday - Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ Record request #26-55 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Syd Jones, The City of Watsonville (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on January 26, 2026 (“Request”).    The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act (“PRA”). (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for the record, that record is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the record is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort.The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions, requests for information, or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. On February 6, 2026, the City requested an additional 14 days to determine whether the City possessed records due to the need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. On February 20, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request. On March 13, 2026, the City provided the first batch of records responsive to your Request. The City now provides an additional batch of responsive, non-exempt records. The City redacted camera locations as the public interest in withholding clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure to prevent camera tampering or vandalism and because the cameras are law enforcement investigatory tools. (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000.)                 With this letter and production, the City considers your Request closed.  If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact us at cityclerk@watsonville.gov City Clerk's Office (831) 768-3010 275 Main Street, Suite 400, Watsonville, CA 95076 FAX: 831-761-0736 E-mail:  cityclerk@watsonville.gov Open Monday - Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? title: Flock Solar Panel - Property of NCIRC (Santa Cruz Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Solar Panel - Property of NCIRC (Santa Cruz Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

communications.subject: Public Records Response
communications.communication:
RESPONSE TO PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST – CITY CLERK
(CA. Gov’t Code §7920.000 et seq.)


Re: PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST RECEIVED ON ­Tuesday, December 23, 2025

The City of Santa Cruz responds to your public records request for information received on ­Tuesday, December 23, 2025, as indicated below.

Under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) you are entitled to copies of identifiable, non-exempt public records (Govt. Code section 7922.500). Please note that the CPRA requires the City to provide access to, or copies of, records responsive to your request which are in its possession, subject to certain exceptions. The CPRA does not require the City to provide information, answer questions, or create records which do not exist.

Records Requested:
“There is a solar panel powering a Flock ALPR camera located at Lat/Long 36.9677, -122.0186, the Northeast corner of 3rd Street and Riverside Ave in Santa Cruz, California. On that solar panel is a sticker that reads: "Property of NCRIC / NCHIDTA", "Flock Solar Panel", "10682". Please provide all contracts, MSAs, MOUs, NDAs, notes, emails and other documents associated with that solar panel.”

The City has extended its time to respond to your request to ­Monday, January 26, 2026 pursuant to the California Public Records Act, Government section 7920.000 et seq. (the “CPRA”), for the following reason:

☒ Government Code § 7922.535(c)(2) • The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.

We appreciate your patience while we thoroughly process your request. Please do not hesitate to contact us with any questions.

Thank you,

Brittany Inclan
Records Coordinator
City of Santa Cruz | City Manager’s Office | City Clerk’s Division
809 Center Street, Santa Cruz, CA 95060
Direct: 831-420-5034
Email: binclan@santacruzca.gov
Web: santacruzca.gov<https://www.cityofsantacruz.com/>
[cid:image001.png@01DC83E6.34828D00]

Public Records Act Requests may be submitted online via the Public Records Request form or by hard copy form available at the City Clerk’s Office located at 809 Center Street, Room 8, Santa Cruz, CA 95060.

Please note: Public Record Act Requests submitted via email, fax, USPS, or drop off after 5:00 p.m. on a business day, Saturdays, Sundays, or holidays will be processed as received on the next open business day. The 10-day response period begins when the request is received.

title: SNAP status

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: SNAP status
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All official communications — including but not limited to emails, memos, letters, text messages, meeting notes, and internal reports — concerning changes to Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as a result of H.R.1 - One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed on July 4, 2025 from July 4, 2025 to the date of this record request. This request specifically includes communications relating to:
-Policy changes
-Program administration or implementation
-Eligibility determinations or rule changes
-Oversight, audits, or funding of SNAP
-Communications with federal agencies or contractors regarding SNAP

If feasible, please include communications sent to or from:
-Officials or staff within the Arizona Department of Economic Security
-Arizona DES Director Michael Wisehart
-SNAP Division leadership or staff
-Any relevant communications between IDHS and the USDA Food and Nutrition Service (FNS)

If these records exist in electronic format, I prefer to receive them electronically via email or download link. Please provide the records in searchable PDF format if possible.
If this request will result in charges, please provide a cost estimate before fulfilling it. 

If you determine that any portion of this request is exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation citing the specific exemption(s) and inform me of my rights to appeal.
Thank you for your attention to this request. I look forward to your response within the ten business days required by law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samantha Liss

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fdepartment-of-economic-security-5983%252Fsnap-status-193810%252F%253Femail%253DPublicRecordsRequest%252540azdes.gov&url_auth_token=AAATdPDAEJsvWHFj7Zne5sHm52c%3A1v3Rkr%3Ao0DxBTY4akrIPm6TaYwfckyWAL-h6sF0_S53yos3Okk

If this request is for commercial purposes or if the request is for electronic records to be printed or provided via CD/DVD, processing fees may apply.  You will be notified via email of any processing fees associated with your request, along with the ways in which the fees can be paid.  DES does not charge applicants or recipients of DES services for copies of their program records. 
PLEASE NOTE: Arizona law does not require DES to create records that do not already exist or are not regularly generated, including tallying and compiling aggregate information. The law also does not require DES to produce an electronic record in a format other than the existing format. 
To view the details or contact DES regarding this request please log into your My Records Center on the DES Public Records Request Portal. You will receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Thank you for using the DES Public Records Request Portal.

communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office, Jason Maulucci, Director of Policy Dev & Legislative Affairs, Return to Office Communications
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Galbraith

This email is to confirm receipt of your request below. Please know that we are making a good faith effort to complete your request as timely as possible and I expect to have it completed this afternoon or tomorrow.

Thank you for your patience.
Karen

communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office, Jason Maulucci, Director of Policy Dev & Legislative Affairs, Return to Office Communications
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Galbraith -

In response to your request below, except as set forth herein, attached please find records responsive to your request.

Certain personal information has been redacted to the extent it is exempt from disclosure in accordance with 1 V.S.A. § 315(a) as a matter of respecting personal privacy.


Also, certain records have been withheld to the extent they are exempt from disclosure under 1 V.S.A. § 317(c)(4) as executive privileged. This includes weekly and legislative reports advisory to the Governor and policy and strategy materials on matters relating to return to office.

Jaye Pershing Johnson, the Governor's Legal Counsel, is responsible for denial of the privileged and confidential records. I conferred with Ms. Johnson when making the determination to redact or withhold information.

As the records custodian, I certify, as required by 1 V.S.A. 318(a)(2), that I consider the redacted and withheld materials to be exempt from disclosure. You have the right to appeal, as explained in 1 V.S.A. 318, to Jason Gibbs, Chief of Staff, relative to my certification of the records as exempt.

Sincerely,
Karen Pallas
Records Officer

title: Request on Political Appointees (Federal Communications Commission)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (Federal Communications Commission)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. These records are likely in the possession of the Designated Agency Ethics Official (DAEO).

Please provide these records as available on a rolling basis.

Documented is an investigative watchdog and reporting group that explores the relationship between special interests and government. This request is likely to contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the operations of the government. Disclosure of the requested records serves the public interest, and this request is not being made for any commercial purposes. Therefore, I am requesting a waiver of any fees. If you do not grant my fee waiver request, please notify me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically by email attachment or other electronic file transfer if available, or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Nina

title: Records Request (Waterbury Town Clerk)

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request (Waterbury Town Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to [the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law], I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP # 8320

Title/Description: Automated Traffic Enforcement System 

Issuing Entity: The City of Waterbury Police Department

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

communications.subject: City - FOI Request :: R004592-031526
communications.communication:
Dear Jon Smith: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of City of Waterbury. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the Connecticut General Statutes. Your request was received in this office on 3/15/2026 and given the reference number R004592-031526 for tracking purposes. 
 Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Pursuant to [the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law], I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP # 8320 

Title/Description: Automated Traffic Enforcement System 

Issuing Entity: The City of Waterbury Police Department 

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment. 

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Jon Smith 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2240506/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Connecticut General Statutes do not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Freedom of Information Center. 
City of Waterbury

title: TOHAS Placement Partners

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: TOHAS Placement Partners
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1 - List of all Placement Partners, including their contact information (name, phone, email), and status (Active, Inactive, Terminated, Suspended). 

2 - Records related to the criteria, qualifications and documentation required for organizations to become Placement Partners.

3 - Records related to the number of animals transferred to each Placement Partners in the past year. 

4 - Contact information for the Director, Placement Partners Coordinator and the Enforcement Offices.

5 - Any complaints or issues that have arisen with your Placement Partners.

6 - Standard Operating Procedures and related documentation for Placement Partners

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Colon

title: NCAA Financial Report (Longwood University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NCAA Financial Report (Longwood University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern,

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009 & 2024.  Specifically:  

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 30+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

If your department does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian’s name and address.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely.  Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Longwood University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Longwood University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: NY DOT: Individual Inspection Records

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NY DOT: Individual Inspection Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request individual-level inspection records for all inspections conducted by NYSDOT's Passenger Carrier Safety Bureau and Motor Carrier Compliance Bureau for the past 5 years (2021-2026), including:

Inspection date
Operator / carrier name and Operator ID
Vehicle identification (VIN, plate number, vehicle type)
Inspection location (facility, roadside, or terminal)
Inspection result (pass / fail)
Deficiencies found (if any) with category and description
Out-of-service orders issued (if any)
Inspector ID or facility ID
Re-inspection date and result (if applicable)

I understand this is a large dataset. I am requesting a structured database export in CSV or pipe-delimited format. If the full 5-year span is too large for a single response, I am willing to receive the data in annual increments.

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #R010047-021626
communications.communication:
Thank you for the responsive records received today. The Carrier Certification System data (active, suspended and revoked authority) and the individual inspection records (R010047, 11 files covering January 2021 through February 2026) are received and acknowledged.                                

I have one clarification on the inspection records. The R010047 files contain bus and passenger vehicle inspections conducted at operator facilities. Each record joins to an Operator Number assigned to passenger carriers (charter, common, contract). No equivalent inspection records for truck or freight carriers appear in this delivery. 

Could you confirm whether NYSDOT maintains a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers? If so, I request those records under the same FOIL request. If NYSDOT does not conduct facility-level inspections for freight    carriers (as distinct from FMCSA roadside inspections), a brief confirmation of that would close this item.

All other responsive records in this delivery appear complete. Thank you for  the prompt response!

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #R010047-021626
communications.communication:
After inspecting the files, I was hoping you could clarify the data:

2025 inspection data appears truncated. The R010047 files for both halves of 2025 contain exactly 100,000 rows each. The H1 file ends on 24-06-2025 (missing 25-30 June) and the H2 file ends on 22-12-2025 (missing 23-31 December). Prior years range from 79,000 to 90,000 rows per half-year, which suggests a row limit was applied during export. Could you re-export both 2025 files without the 100,000-row cap?

Thank you again for your responsiveness.

communications.subject: New Message :: R010047-021626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Ian White, 
On 3/18/2026 11:36 you wrote: 
"Thank you for the responsive records received today. The Carrier Certification System data (active, suspended and revoked authority) and the individual inspection records (R010047, 11 files covering January 2021 through February 2026) are received and acknowledged. I have one clarification on the inspection records. The R010047 files contain bus and passenger vehicle inspections conducted at operator facilities. Each record joins to an Operator Number assigned to passenger carriers (charter, common, contract). No equivalent inspection records for truck or freight carriers appear in this delivery. Could you confirm whether NYSDOT maintains a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers? If so, I request those records under the same FOIL request. If NYSDOT does not conduct facility-level inspections for freight carriers (as distinct from FMCSA roadside inspections), a brief confirmation of that would close this item. All other responsive records in this delivery appear complete. Thank you for the prompt response! After inspecting the files, I was hoping you could clarify the data: 2025 inspection data appears truncated. The R010047 files for both halves of 2025 contain exactly 100, 000 rows each. The H1 file ends on 24-06-2025 (missing 25-30 June) and the H2 file ends on 22-12-2025 (missing 23-31 December). Prior years range from 79, 000 to 90, 000 rows per half-year, which suggests a row limit was applied during export. Could you re-export both 2025 files without the 100, 000-row cap? Thank you again for your responsiveness. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2244206/" 
With respect to the portion of your inquiry asking about " a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers", a diligent search of our files failed to reveal any records that are responsive to your request.  To the extent that you are also seeking a statement of information regarding our agency's functions, FOIL is specific to access of agency records and requests for information, answers to questions, or explanations of records provided are not contemplated by the law.  The Committee on Open Government, the New York State agency which provides advisory opinions on FOIL matters, has stated in opinion FOIL-AO-13208, “I note that the title of the [F]reedom of Information Law may be somewhat misleading, for it is not a vehicle that requires agencies to provide information per se; rather, it requires agencies to disclose records to the extent provided by law.” 
With respect to the portion of your inquiry seeking a " re-export both 2025 files without the 100,000-row cap", thank you for making me aware of this specific issue. I have reached out to the program area to request that they repull the 2025 files with an eye towards assuring that the data is not limited by a row cap.  I will release those records to you once the program area has made them available to me. Your records will be released via this, our Records Access Center -- we will not upload documents to an external website. 
Thank you kindly, 
Jonathan Allen 
Records Access Officer 
The New York State Department of Transportation

title: Request to Marines for "Big Rocks Meeting" Communication Director briefs, from June 2025 to present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Marines for "Big Rocks Meeting" Communication Director briefs, from June 2025 to present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All "Big Rocks Meeting" Communication Directorate briefs of the US Marines from June 11, 2025 to the present. The Marines previously released to me, via FOIA, a collection of such briefs dated from January 2022 until June 10, 2025.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Your FOIA Request 2026-001850
communications.communication:
Mr. Carollo - This is to acknowledge the receipt of your requests and to let you know that I am the analyst assigned for processing.  If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me or our office. 

Please be aware that due to the government shutdown we are still ‘catching up’ and there may be some significant delays in receiving a response.  Please note that we are doing our best and are working diligently to complete all incoming requests. 

Please also be aware that, whenever a FOIA requester has not received a determination within 20 working days from the date a request is perfected, they are entitled to challenge the delay.  Appeals may be submitted within the SecureRelease online portal at www.securerelease.us or by writing to the Department of the Navy, Office of the Judge Advocate General (Code 14), 1322 Patterson Ave SE, Suite 3000, Washington Navy Yard, DC 20374-5066.  If you prefer, you can also submit an appeal via e-mail to OJAG-C14-FOIA-PA-ADMINAPPEALS@us.navy.mil.  Your appeal, if any, should include a statement indicating why you believe it should be granted. 

You also have the right to seek assistance and/or dispute resolution services from the Department of the Navy FOIA Public Liaison, Mr. Christopher Julka, at (703) 697-0031 or christopher.a.julka.civ@us.navy.mil.  You may also contact the Office of Government Information Services for assistance at ogis@nara.gov or (877) 684-6448. 

Sincerely, 

Deanna James 
FOIA Analyst

title: Epstein Investigation (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein Investigation (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I request access to all non-exempt records, documents, and evidence in your possession relating to investigations of Jeffrey Edward Epstein (DOB: January 20, 1953), including but not limited to:

1. Flight logs from Epstein’s private aircraft (including aircraft tail numbers N908JE, N212JE, and N474AA), dated from January 1, 1995, to August 10, 2019.


2. Inventories and logs of physical and digital evidence seized during law enforcement raids of the following Epstein properties:

9 East 71st Street, Manhattan, NY

Little St. James Island, U.S. Virgin Islands

Palm Beach mansion, FL

Zorro Ranch, NM



3. Any itemized lists, reports, or media inventories documenting the contents of:

CDs, hard drives, or other storage devices labeled or cataloged by law enforcement

Surveillance footage recorded inside Epstein's residences or private aircraft



4. Chain-of-custody documentation for any seized videos, digital drives, or photographic evidence.


5. Correspondence between the FBI and the Department of Justice or other government agencies regarding the management, review, or disposition of the aforementioned evidence.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Justin Griggs

accessed.

You can renew the download files by logging into the efoia system and clicking
the "Renew" button in the file list.

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

You may check the status of your FOIPA request on the FBIâ€™s electronic FOIA
Library (The Vault) on the FBIâ€™s public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by
clicking on the "Check the Status of Your FOI/PA Request tool" link. Status
updates are performed on a weekly basis.
